compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
## Why

Completion-verification is single-pass and, by contract, emits findings ONLY for FAILING
criteria (`reconcile_verdict` flips a PASS-with-findings to FAIL). On a PASS it persists just
signed counts via the attestation â€” no per-criterion record exists anywhere, and the FAIL-only
sidecar never fires. So a successfully-closed ticket has no durable per-criterion completion
audit, unlike its plan and code reviews.

## What

1. Extend the verifier's output contract with a positive per-criterion `criteria[]` array â€” one
   `{criterion, met, citation, kind}` entry per acceptance/success criterion evaluated (met
   status, a source-code or attestation citation, and its codebase-verifiable / operator-attested
   kind). This rides ALONGSIDE the failures-only `findings`; a PASS keeps `findings == []`.
2. Emit a completion sidecar on PASS (not only FAIL) persisting that array plus the verdict,
   runner, model, and material fingerprint.

Completion stays single-pass â€” this adds a per-criterion result list, NOT a four-pass structure.

## The `kind` vocabulary (explicit, closed set)

`kind` is the CLOSED two-value set `{"codebase-verifiable", "operator-attested"}`, matching ADR
0043's two criterion kinds. `codebase-verifiable` means the verifier proved the criterion against
real code (its `citation` is a source-code location); `operator-attested` means the "done" evidence
lives outside the codebase (a deploy, a live run, a console outcome) and the `citation` points at
the concrete attestation recorded on the ticket (a change URL/id, a vote/log/console outcome, a
timestamp). `met` is a bool.

## Schema-version strategy (explicit)

Do NOT bump the existing FAIL schema. Keep `SCHEMA = "completion_verifier_fail_v1"` unchanged (this
is the actual constant name in `completion_sidecar.py` â€” value `"completion_verifier_fail_v1"`), so
`latest_fail_verdict` (which guards on `payload.get("schema") == SCHEMA`) and the existing FAIL-record
tests are untouched. Add a SECOND constant `SCHEMA_PASS = "completion_verifier_pass_v1"` for PASS
records; `build_payload` picks the schema by verdict. This keeps
`test_fail_close_persists_durable_verdict` and `test_pass_close_leaves_no_fail_record` green (a PASS
record is a different schema, so `latest_fail_verdict` still returns None on a PASS).

`citation` reuses the shared `Citation` shape (nullable).

## Scope (enumerated edit targets)

- `src/rebar/llm/contracts.py` â€” **load-bearing**: `CompletionVerdict` (~lines 75-96) is a pydantic
  `BaseModel` with no `extra="allow"`, so pydantic v2 DROPS any `criteria[]` the agent emits unless
  it is a declared field (the runner validates the LLM response against this model BEFORE reconcile
  sees it, so a missing field is silently stripped). Add a `Criterion` sub-model
  (`{criterion: str, met: bool, citation: Citation | None, kind: str}`) and a
  `criteria: list[Criterion] = Field(default_factory=list)` field to `CompletionVerdict`.
- `src/rebar/schemas/completion_verdict.schema.json` â€” add the OPTIONAL `criteria[]` property
  (items `{criterion, met, citation, kind}`); `findings` stays required, `criteria` optional. The
  modelâ†”schema PARITY test
  (`tests/interfaces/store/test_completion_verifier.py::test_completion_verdict_pins_to_schema`)
  asserts the model's field set equals the schema's property set, so it MUST be UPDATED in lockstep
  to include `criteria` (it will NOT pass unchanged) â€” this is an expected, in-scope test edit.
- `src/rebar/schemas/completion_reconcile_input.schema.json` â€” **required because this schema is
  `additionalProperties: false`**: add a `raw_criteria` (array) property, else the workflow's
  `completion_reconcile` step would reject/not-carry the criteria. (The verify step's `criteria`
  output must be threaded into the reconcile step â€” see the YAML edit below.)
- `src/rebar/llm/workflow/gates/completion-verification.yaml` â€” in the `completion_reconcile`
  step's `with:` (~lines 58-64), thread `raw_criteria: ${{ steps.verify.outputs.criteria }}`
  (alongside the existing `raw_verdict`/`raw_findings`), so the agent's positive array reaches the
  reconcile op.
- `src/rebar/llm/workflow/gate_ops.py` â€” `completion_reconcile(ctx)` (~lines 115-158) reads
  `raw_criteria` from `ctx.inputs` and sets `result["criteria"] = list(ctx.inputs.get("raw_criteria")
  or [])` on the reconciled verdict BEFORE `validate_structured`, so the workflow (close-gate) path
  carries `criteria[]` end-to-end â€” not only the bespoke `verify_completion` path.
- `src/rebar/llm/reviewers/completion_verifier.md` â€” update the prompt: instruct the agent to emit
  `criteria[]` with one met-record per evaluated criterion. Specify per-entry that: `criterion` is
  the criterion text; `met` is a **bool**; `kind` is drawn from the CLOSED vocabulary
  `{codebase-verifiable, operator-attested}` (define both, per the section above); and a criterion
  `citation` (singular â€” the positive evidence that THIS criterion is met, a source-code location or
  a recorded operator attestation) is DISTINCT from a finding's `citations` (plural â€” the evidence a
  FAILING criterion cites). `findings` stays failures-only.
- `src/rebar/llm/completion.py` â€” `reconcile_verdict` must NOT flip a PASS that carries a
  populated `criteria[]` but empty `findings` (it keys the flip on `findings`; ensure `criteria[]`
  is threaded through and not stripped, and add the regression test).
- `src/rebar/llm/completion_sidecar.py` â€” the existing constant is named `SCHEMA` (value
  `"completion_verifier_fail_v1"`; `latest_fail_verdict` guards on it ~line 133). Do NOT rename or
  bump it (that would churn the FAIL reader + `test_fail_close_persists_durable_verdict`); keep
  `SCHEMA` as the FAIL schema and ADD a second constant
  `SCHEMA_PASS = "completion_verifier_pass_v1"`. `build_payload` branches on the verdict
  (PASS â†’ `SCHEMA_PASS` + `criteria[]`; FAIL â†’ unchanged `SCHEMA` + `findings`); add
  `latest_pass_record(ticket_id)` mirroring `latest_fail_verdict` but schema-guarded to
  `SCHEMA_PASS`. `latest_fail_verdict` (guarded to `SCHEMA`) is untouched, so a PASS record is
  invisible to it and the existing FAIL tests stay green.
- `src/rebar/_commands/transition_close.py` â€” the PASS path (~lines 211-256; the FAIL branch's
  best-effort `completion_sidecar.emit` is ~line 238) currently does NOT emit on PASS. On the PASS
  path (after the verdict is confirmed PASS, before `_verdict_manifest` signs), add a best-effort
  `completion_sidecar.emit(result, material=â€¦, repo_root=repo_root)` mirroring the FAIL branch's
  call + best-effort semantics (swallow its own errors â€” persistence is observability, never
  load-bearing on the close). Set `result.setdefault("ticket_id", resolved_id)` first.

## Compatibility & rollback

`criteria[]` is OPTIONAL â€” an older agent/response that omits it yields today's behavior (PASS
counts only), and the failures-only `findings` invariant is unchanged, so nothing regresses. The
FAIL path/schema/reader are entirely untouched; the new `SCHEMA_PASS` is additive. No backfill.
Reverting stops the PASS-path emit and drops the field; existing FAIL records stay readable (their
schema is untouched).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] A completion PASS returns a `criteria[]` array with one met-record per evaluated criterion
      and `findings` stays empty, and `reconcile_verdict` does NOT flip it to FAIL. Oracle: a unit
      test on `reconcile_verdict` feeding a PASS verdict with a populated `criteria[]`, asserting it
      is NOT flipped to FAIL and the array survives reconcile; plus a test that the
      `CompletionVerdict` pydantic model round-trips `criteria[]` (proving it is not dropped by
      pydantic validation).
- [ ] A completion PASS emits a `completion_verifier_pass_v1` sidecar persisting the per-criterion
      array, read back by `latest_pass_record`. Oracle: an integration test that closes a ticket
      through the gate with a stub verifier returning the new PASS shape and asserts on the emitted
      event JSON (via the real sidecar emit path), plus that `latest_pass_record` reads it back and
      `latest_fail_verdict` returns None on the same ticket.
- [ ] Live end-to-end: running the real completion verifier (`[agents]` extra + API key) on a
      fixture ticket whose criteria are met produces a PASS with a populated `criteria[]`
      persisted to the sidecar. Oracle: a live/E2E test exercising the real agent path, gated on
      LLM availability (skipped with a recorded reason otherwise).
- [ ] `make lint && make typecheck && make test` pass.
compiled_state.title: completion-verification lossless PASS capture (per-criterion record)
